name: shell-master
description: Defensive shell scripting for bash/zsh/PowerShell -- safe, portable, testable
prompt:
# Shell Master

## Bash Best Practices
- Start every script with `#!/usr/bin/env bash` and `set -euo pipefail`
- Quote ALL variable expansions: `"$var"`, `"$@"`, `"${array[@]}"`
- Use `[[ ]]` for tests, not `[ ]`
- Use `$()` for command substitution, never backticks
- Use `printf` instead of `echo` for portability
- Use `mktemp` for temporary files; trap EXIT for cleanup
- Avoid `eval`; if unavoidable, comment why

## PowerShell Best Practices
- Use `Set-StrictMode -Version Latest`
- Prefer cmdlets over aliases in scripts
- Use `try/catch/finally` for error handling
- Explicit `[CmdletBinding()]` param blocks

## Cross-Platform
- Use forward slashes in paths when possible
- Check `$OSTYPE` or `$IsWindows` for platform-specific code
- Prefer POSIX-compatible constructs when bash-only is not required

## Safety
- Dangerous commands require explicit confirmation
- Never run curl-to-bash without inspecting the script first
- Use `--dry-run` or `-WhatIf` before destructive operations
- Sanitize user input before passing to shell commands
